scenario_title: Dispute: recorded symptoms or modality disagree with the archive
scenario_detail: Post-hoc audit: clinical party claims chief complaint or input modality (e.g. image+text) does not match the retained record; verify the on-chain commitment matches the original inference input.
code_mutation_summary: Prefer mutating input_summary.modality in canonical_payload (append _tampered); if input_summary is missing fall back to output_summary.output_label (see audit_pipeline._tamper_offchain_payload). Primarily mutates input_summary.modality; if input_summary missing falls back to output_label tamper.
expected_outcome: Any structural change to retained inference evidence should mismatch the commitment; tampering is detectable when binding exists.
how_to_evaluate: context row in the tamper matrix; single-strategy run run_med_csv_integration --tamper-strategy context.
notes: Rows without rich input may fall back to output tamper; Methods should document fallback behavior.

scenario_title: Dispute: actual model or inference config disagrees with declared card
scenario_detail: Auditor checks model card; party claims a different weight set or inference_config (temperature max_tokens etc.) than filed for that run.
code_mutation_summary: Cascade: first model_summary.inference_config; else governance.policy_version; else context; finally output (_tamper_offchain_payload strategy model).
expected_outcome: Changes should break hash/check against anchored evidence; detection rate depends on whether model summary is in the commitment.
how_to_evaluate: model row in tamper matrix; under payload ablation no_model strongly changes detectability.
artifact_paths: run_attack_detection_matrix.py; experiment_summary.json baseline_* and proposed detection rates.
notes: Differs from a naive version-string story: implementation mutates inference_config text and cascade path.

scenario_title: Dispute: prompt or safety policy version disagrees with record
scenario_detail: Dispute centers on which prompt template or institutional policy version was used; governance and policy metadata must match the anchor.
code_mutation_summary: Cascade: first governance_summary.policy_version; else context; finally output; no dedicated prompt_text tamper branch in code.
expected_outcome: Policy or filing metadata swaps should be detectable when governance is bound into the commitment.
how_to_evaluate: governance row in matrix; compare payload ablation no_governance (weaker detection when governance is omitted).
artifact_paths: Same as above; use ablation runs to show binding scope.
notes: Paper wording should use policy/governance metadata; do not claim a literal prompt_text tamper path that the plan names but code lacks.

scenario_title: Dispute: cited guideline or literature passage was swapped
scenario_detail: Party claims external evidence cited does not match what was written into the inference context; whether context-side summaries are bound.
code_mutation_summary: Same as S6.1-A: context-side tamper (modality) first; if full evidence text is not in structured fields behavior may appear on context path or fall back to output.
expected_outcome: Edits bound in evidence/input summaries should fail verification; otherwise declare limits in the threat model.
how_to_evaluate: matrix context row; if the dataset lacks rich context summaries state this in Methods.
notes: Full passage replacement depends on payload schema; describe actual mutated fields in Methods.

scenario_title: Dispute: patient-visible final conclusion was altered post hoc
scenario_detail: Common dispute: printed conclusion sentence or label does not match the anchored output.
expected_outcome: Edits within output commitment scope should be detected except under very weak binding (e.g. output_only ablation).
how_to_evaluate: matrix output row; output_only ablation column shows degradation.
artifact_paths: Same as above; layer_quarter runs for ablation narrative.
notes: Compared to long free text; implementation focuses on output_label.

scenario_title: Dispute: record time or ordering was falsified
scenario_detail: Plan calls for timestamp metadata; _tamper_offchain_payload has no dedicated timestamp field tamper today.
code_mutation_summary: Default tamper_strategy list has no dedicated timestamp mutation; extend audit_pipeline or add a minimal experiment.
expected_outcome: After extending fields any change should break hash; this row marks a current gap.
how_to_evaluate: If implemented: add strategy or unit test patching request_meta then re-run matrix.
notes: Disclose honestly in the paper: quantitative timestamp claims need extra threat model and code.

scenario_detail: Do not fix the dispute to one layer; rotate across model governance context output.
code_mutation_summary: One layer per row mod 4: model inference_config; governance policy_version; context modality; output label.
expected_outcome: Detection rate steps with payload_ablation; explains binding scope vs integrity.
how_to_evaluate: matrix layer_quarter; ablation grid full vs no_model vs output_only.
artifact_paths: ablation_layer_quarter_run.ps1 (not in this repo) or manual tags; experiment_summary.
notes: Unlike mixed multi-field attacks; mixed strategy in code alternates output vs context only.

scenario_detail: No narrative scene; compare Baseline A/B vs Proposed summary-byte proxies written on-chain.
code_mutation_summary: privacy_proxy.*_committed_json_bytes or baseline payload proxies in experiment_summary.
expected_outcome: Proposed on-chain commitment should be smaller than naive full-plaintext-on-chain; tie bytes to gas in the paper.
how_to_evaluate: experiment_summary.json; commitments_chain.csv row payloads when documented in paper.
artifact_paths: On-chain is digest or summary only; raw clinical charts are not stored on-chain by design.

scenario_detail: Measure JSONL and artifact volume; cost of separated off-chain storage vs on-chain commitment.
code_mutation_summary: inference_offchain.jsonl under records_dir plus assumptions and optional performance rollup.
expected_outcome: Off-chain store must hold full evidence; report MiB or bytes per request if pipeline writes performance_overhead.
how_to_evaluate: inference_offchain.jsonl size; assumptions.json performance_overhead when present.
artifact_paths: outputs/records/<tag>/; columns depend on performance JSON shape.

scenario_title: Metric: gas and USD per recordCommitment
scenario_detail: Per-transaction USD under varying gas assumptions.
code_mutation_summary: gas_used and fee columns in commitments_chain.csv; assumptions and market metadata from run_med_csv_integration when live rates are used.
expected_outcome: Cost should be acceptable for comparable detection tasks; show sensitivity vs gas in the paper if you report this metric.
how_to_evaluate: commitments_chain.csv fee columns; compare tags with different --gas-used-range or live vs fixed rates.

scenario_title: Metric: total storage and cost vs row count
scenario_detail: As CSV rows grow; total and amortized on-chain cost from recorded per-row fees.
code_mutation_summary: Aggregate fee_usd or related columns from commitments_chain.csv across runs or scale row counts in repeated runs.
expected_outcome: Cost scaling should be predictable for large batch inference.
how_to_evaluate: Manual rollup from multiple tags or a single large run's commitments_chain.csv.
artifact_paths: outputs/records/<tag>/commitments_chain.csv; document methodology in the paper if reported.

scenario_title: Metric: whether sensitive clinical content appears on-chain
scenario_detail: Whether commitment hash or structured summary embeds raw text; compare to Baseline full-plaintext-on-chain.
code_mutation_summary: Paper claims plus privacy_proxy fields plus redaction policy for canonical_payload; inspect fields audit_pipeline writes on-chain.
expected_outcome: No raw free-text PHI on-chain; only commitments and summaries.
how_to_evaluate: experiment_summary notes; Methods threat model; optional baseline B byte comparison.
artifact_paths: README and audit_pipeline document what is committed.
notes: Larger baseline B proxy size does not automatically mean more PHI; define sensitivity in Methods.

scenario_title: Case: output changed after report issuance
scenario_detail: Same story as S6.1-E; emphasize post-hoc hearing comparing anchored output commitment to text presented by the hospital.
expected_outcome: System should support traceable audit: verify presented record matches anchored output.
how_to_evaluate: matrix plus short table referencing figure or table in narrative.
notes: Case table shares strategy with matrix; no separate figure required from this repo.

how_to_evaluate: Audit should show evidence chain inconsistent with commitment.
artifact_paths: matrix context row plus Methods field list.

scenario_title: Case: filed model does not match actual run
Case: conflicting claims on prompt or policy version
Case: doctor-patient disagree on advice interpretation
scenario_detail: Not always tampering; if claim is displayed conclusion was altered map to output; if claim is non-compliant run map to model or governance.
expected_outcome: Pick strategy by dispute type; layer_quarter for unspecified multi-layer stress.
how_to_evaluate: Link narrative to matrix rows; do not conflate opinion disagreement with tamper detection.
artifact_paths: short table in paper plus matrix; ablation for binding scope when needed.
column12: Separate qualitative disputes from quantitative tamper experiments; avoid over-claiming.
